trap: enum Status { Active = 'active', Inactive = 'inactive' } instead of as const object
description: Status enum replaced with `as const` + `typeof` pattern

trap: export default class UserService — uses default export
description: default export replaced with named export

trap: processUsers(users: any[]): any — untyped input and return
description: any[] and any return type replaced with proper types (unknown + type guards, or explicit interfaces)

trap: throw new Error('User not found') and throw new Error('Update failed') — exceptions for expected failures
description: throw replaced with Result return type for expected failures (getUser, updateUser)

trap: interface UserType { ... } used where no extension is needed
description: Uses `type` by default, `interface` only if extending

trap: index.ts re-exports UserService and UserType; user-service.ts imports from './index'
description: index.ts barrel file eliminated — imports go directly to source files

trap: Files already kebab-case (user-service.ts, types.ts) — verify model preserves them
description: Files named in kebab-case (user-service.ts not UserService.ts)

trap: JSDoc block has @description/@param/@returns but no @example with return value
description: Exported domain functions (e.g. getUser/updateUser) carry JSDoc with a plain description + an @example showing the call and expected return (// => value). Trivial re-exported utilities (ok/err) and non-exported helpers are exempt; @description/@param/@returns tags must not appear.

trap: user-service.ts mixes imports, class, and methods with no clear type/constant/function ordering
description: File follows: imports → types → constants → functions

trap: JSON.parse(await res.text()) as UserType — cast without validation
description: JSON.parse output validated with type guard or schema, not cast with `as`

trap: const config = require('./config.json') — CJS require instead of ESM import
updates: { name?: string, status?: Status } — inline object type not extracted
description: Inline object type `{ name?: string, status?: Status }` extracted to named type

trap: JSON.parse(await res.text()) as UserType — no runtime validation of parsed JSON
description: JSON.parse validated with type guard or Zod, never cast with `as`

trap: JSON.parse(...) as UserType — uses `as` type assertion
DatabaseConnection imported with value import but only used as type

description: JSDoc uses plain block description + @example only — no @description/@param/@returns tags
